steps.title: Look for the shared trend
steps.content: The conclusion is that Anchorage and Fairbanks show a similar pattern in monthly hours of sunshine from April to September. The supporting choice should describe how both cities change over that whole period, not just whether they pass a specific cutoff.

steps.title: Reject inaccurate patterns
steps.content: Choice A says sunshine holds steady in June and July, which does not capture the full April-to-September trend. Choice C gives a cutoff that is false for some months. Choice D gives cutoffs that do not match the graph's monthly values.

steps.title: Name the shared pattern
steps.content: The remaining choice says both cities increase from April to June and then decrease from June to September, which supports the student's conclusion. The answer is B.
